globalStyle: Original YjsE Terraria-inspired side-view foliage rendered as one dense overlapping macro canopy with binary alpha, irregular exposed outlines, broad species-specific light and shadow volumes, and no internal tile grid.
referenceUse: Composition direction only. Runtime sheets are deterministic native-size pixel output, not cropped, sampled, traced, or scaled from the reference.
variantContract: Variants A/B/C use deliberately different macro compositions while sharing full connected-edge sockets. Adjacent cardinal connections add stepped corner fills so dense V5 crown lobes form one mass without alpha seams, ring-shaped junction holes, periodic dots, checker patterns, or repeated centered motifs.
requirements: Export exactly sixteen native 16x16 frames in top-right-bottom-left mask order 0-15.
Use broad mask-aware side sockets and connected-corner fills; do not place a dark outline along an internal tile edge.
Keep exposed pad edges irregular with a one-pixel dark contour while center and multi-neighbor frames remain solid foliage mass.
Use binary alpha and broad connected 2-7 pixel color masses; do not use isolated diamond blobs, internal checker holes, periodic dot or diagonal patterns, antialiasing, or raster scaling.

briefs.subject: Connected Oak Crown Mass V2A
briefs.prompt: Author an original broad oak autotile with a left-raised organic contour, solid joined interiors, and softly grouped upper-left highlights.

briefs.subject: Connected Oak Crown Mass V2B
briefs.prompt: Author an original broad oak autotile with a right-weighted organic contour, solid joined interiors, and softly grouped upper-left highlights.

briefs.subject: Connected Oak Crown Mass V2C
briefs.prompt: Author an original broad oak autotile with a lower central organic contour, solid joined interiors, and softly grouped upper-left highlights.
